name: Marc's Christmas Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the sugar, salt, cream, butter (using butter instead of margarine is recommended for this recipe), syrup, and chocolate. Bring to a gentle boil over low heat. Cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture reaches 248 degrees on a candy thermometer or achieves the firm-ball stage when a small amount is dropped into cold water.
2. Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Stir in the vanilla extract and pecan halves. Butter an 8-inch square pan and pour in the caramel. Press 32 cherries, cut in half, onto the caramel if desired. Cool the candy until firm.
3. Cut into 1-inch pieces (resulting in 64 pieces with a cherry half on each), and place each piece in candy paper liners available at cake decorating supply stores or gift shops. Alternatively, wrap each piece in waxed paper.
